ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the movie was released */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre of the movie */,
  "producer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the producer of the movie */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current production status (e.g., pre-production, production, post-production, released) */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the movie */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie record was last updated */,
  "movie_format" TEXT /* Format of the movie (e.g., theatrical, streaming, TV) */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Rating of the movie (e.g., PG-13, R, NR) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_producer_id FOREIGN KEY ("producer_id") REFERENCES producers ("producer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., Comedy, Drama) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "genre_category" TEXT /* Category of the genre (e.g., action, comedy, drama) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the genre record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the genre record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "producers" (
  "producer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each producer */,
  "producer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the producer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the producer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the producer */,
  "company" TEXT /* Production company the producer represents */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the producer record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the producer record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("producer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the actor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the actor */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the agent representing the actor */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the actor (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the actor */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* Profile picture of the actor */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography of the actor */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the actor record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the actor record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actors_agent_id FOREIGN KEY ("agent_id") REFERENCES agents ("agent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "agents" (
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each agent */,
  "agent_name" TEXT /* Full name of the agent */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the agent */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the agent */,
  "company" TEXT /* Agency the agent represents */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the agent record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the agent record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("agent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the character */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the character (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "character_type" TEXT /* Type of character (e.g., protagonist, antagonist, supporting) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the character record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the character record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_characters" (
  "movie_character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-character relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of role (e.g., Leading, Supporting) */,
  "billing_order" INTEGER /* Billing order of the character in the movie */,
  "billing_type" TEXT /* Type of billing (e.g., leading, supporting, cameo) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie-character relationship record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie-character relationship record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_characters_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_characters_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_actors" (
  "movie_actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-actor relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of role (e.g., Leading, Supporting) */,
  "billing_order" INTEGER /* Billing order of the actor in the movie */,
  "billing_type" TEXT /* Type of billing (e.g., leading, supporting, cameo) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie-actor relationship record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie-actor relationship record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_actors_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_actors_character_id FOREIGN KEY ("character_id") REFERENCES characters ("character_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_actors_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-genre relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie-genre relationship record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the movie-genre relationship record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_stages" (
  "production_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production stage */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "stage" TEXT /* Stage of production (e.g., pre-production, production, post-production) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the production stage */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the production stage */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the production stage (e.g., planned, in progress, completed) */,
  "budget_allocation" REAL /* Budget allocated to the production stage */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the production stage record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the production stage record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("production_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_stages_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., producer, director, casting agent) */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team the user belongs to (e.g., production, casting, marketing) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access log record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access log record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
